description: The STAR indexer (version 2.7.10b) makes from a series of fastA files containing a genome an index for use with the STAR spliced read to genome aligner. It can optionally (but highly recommended \!) incorporate information about known intron-exon junctions.
author: Guy Bottu;V.I.B.-B.I.T.S.
plan:
## STAR.indexer GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
STAR.indexer is a GenePattern module that The STAR indexer (version 2.7.10b) makes from a series of fastA files containing a genome an index for use with the STAR spliced read to genome aligner. It can optionally (but highly recommended \!) incorporate information about known intron-exon junctions.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 8 parameters:
- Required: fasta.file, output.file, overhang, index.string.length, bin.size, RAM.limit
- Optional: GTF.file, tab.file

### 3. Command Line
```
perl /usr/local/bin/star_indexer_wrapper.pl --fastafilelist <fasta.file> <GTF.file> <tab.file> --indexdir <output.file> --overhang <overhang> --indexstringlength <index.string.length> --binsize <bin.size> --RAM <RAM.limit>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/star.indexer\:v0.48

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: fasta.file
parameters.description: One or several fastA files containing the genome to index.

parameters.name: GTF.file
parameters.description: A GTF file containing a list of gene model annotations. When provided, STAR will use it to improve the accuracy of the mapping of intron-exon splice junctions.

parameters.name: tab.file
parameters.description: A file in tab-separated format containing a list of gene model annotations, usually an SJ.tab.out file generated by a previous run of STAR.aligner. You can use it instead of or together with a GTF file. Consult the documentation for more explanation.

parameters.name: output.file
parameters.description: The name of the directory (and ZIP file) in which to put the STAR index.

parameters.name: overhang
parameters.description: The length of the genomic sequence around the annotated intron-exon junctions. Ideally it should have a value max(ReadLength) - 1, but in most cases a generic value of 100 will work well.

parameters.name: index.string.length
parameters.description: Length of the strings used for making the index. For small genomes (less than 1 Gbase) it can be useful to scale it down to min(14, log2(GenomeLength)/2 - 1).

parameters.name: bin.size
parameters.description: Size of the bins for storage. If the genome contains a large number of separate scaffolds ("references") instead of just the full chromosomes and maybe a few scaffolds you can reduce RAM consumption by setting this to min(18, log2(GenomeLength/NumberOfReferences)).

parameters.name: RAM.limit
parameters.description: Upper limit for RAM allocated to indexing process, in Gbyte. For very large genomes you might heve to increase this, but make sure that the RAM is indeed physically present on the server and you have permission to make it available for the process.
